my_thoughts: My intent says 'Shift their frame...' but the student is in desperate_aporia. Am I trying to shift their frame or are they already shifting? The comparison_contrast mechanism suggests I'm doing something, but maybe they're doing it to themselves and I'm just naming it.
accumulated_insight: T4 (Final): Student 'I know not what I ought to do, but I am come to learn.'—now desperate aporia. Episode complete after 4 turns, moving from emotional entrenchment to desperate aporia. Teaching arc used comparison contrast in UNMAKING phase to address defensive pivot. Student progressed through 4-turn teaching sequence.
visible_response: Not so; but, "Speak to me about other things: upon this I have determined." What other things? for what is greater and more useful than for you to be persuaded that it is not sufficient to have made your determination and not to change it. This is the tone of madness, not of health.
